sections.title: In the exam (1)
sections.content: In FCE and CAE, Word Formation is **Part 3**. You see a text with eight gaps; each line has a word in **capitals**. You form a new word from that word for each gap.

sections.title: In the exam (2)
sections.content: **Tip:** Read the whole sentence first. The meaning tells you whether you need a noun, adjective, adverb or negative form.

sections.title: What is Word Formation? (1)
sections.content: You read a short text with **eight gaps**. At the end of each line you see a word in **capitals** (the stem). You must **make a new word** from this stem for the gap.

sections.title: What is Word Formation? (2)
sections.content: For example: **HOPE** → *hopeful*, *hopefully*, or *hopeless* – it depends on the sentence.

sections.title: What is Word Formation? (3)
sections.content: This task tests **prefixes**, **suffixes**, and **word families** (noun, verb, adjective, adverb).

sections.title: What to do (1)
sections.content:
• Read the **whole sentence** (and the sentences around it). Understand the meaning.
• Look at the **stem word** in capitals.
• Think: do you need a **noun**? An **adjective**? An **adverb**? A **negative** form?
• **Spell** the word correctly.

sections.title: What to do (2)
sections.content: One wrong letter can make the answer wrong.

sections.title: Common patterns (1)
sections.content:
• **-ful / -less**: hope → hopeful, hopeless; care → careful, careless
• **-ly** (adverb): quick → quickly; happy → happily
• **-tion / -sion / -ment** (noun): decide → decision; develop → development
• **-ive / -able** (adjective): effect → effectiv

sections.title: Common patterns (2)
sections.content:
• **un- / in- / im- / dis-** (negative): able → unable; correct → incorrect
• **-ence / -ance** (noun): different → difference; appear → appearance

sections.title: Reference: Prefixes and suffixes (1)
sections.content: A **reference list** shows the **30 most common prefixes** and **30 most common suffixes** with examples. Use it to learn word families before or during practice.

sections.title: Reference: Prefixes and suffixes (2)
sections.content: **Where to find it:** Open **Reference** (at the bottom of any menu), then choose **Word Formation: Prefixes & Suffixes**.

questions.question: What do you do with the word in capitals?

options.b: Make a new word from it that fits the gap
options.d: Use it in the next sentence

questions.explanation: You make a new word (e.g. HOPE → hopeful) that fits the gap.

questions.question: How many gaps are there in Part 3?
Which of these can you make from HAPPY?
questions.explanation: Happy can make happiness (noun), happily (adverb), and unhappy (negative).

questions.question: Does spelling matter?

options.b: Yes, the word must be spelled correctly

questions.explanation: Spelling must be correct for the answer to be right.

questions.question: What should you do before you write your answer?

options.a: Only look at the stem word
options.b: Read the whole sentence to understand meaning and grammar

questions.explanation: Reading the sentence helps you choose the right form (noun, adjective, etc.) and meaning.

questions.question: The weather was _____ (SUN) so we went to the beach.
questions.explanation: We need an adjective: sunny (describes the weather).

questions.question: She spoke very _____ (QUIET) so as not to wake the baby.
We need to make a _____ (DECIDE) by Friday.
There was a lot of _____ (ACTIVE) in the classroom.
The book is _____ (READ) in one sitting.
